management_steps.details: Suction, tubes, oxygen, pharmacology, IV access, confirmatory devices, bougie, alternative devices, rescue devices, surgical airway
management_steps.caution: Ensure all equipment functional before starting

management_steps.details: Mallampati score, Measurements (3-3-1 or 3-3-2 rule), Atlanto-occipital extension, Pathology assessment
management_steps.caution: Predict difficult airway using LEMON criteria

management_steps.details: Stretcher height appropriate, Patient at end of bed, Sniff position, Special positioning for obese (ramping) and paediatric patients
100% oxygen for 3-5 minutes via FMO2/BVM/NIV
management_steps.caution: Critically ill patients may need longer pre-oxygenation or NIV

management_steps.details: 10-20ml/kg Balsol/Ringers Lactate
Follow immediately with paralytic agent
management_steps.caution: Be prepared for hypotension with propofol

management_steps.details: Use appropriate blade and technique, consider bougie if predicted difficult airway
Waveform capnography, auscultation, ETCO2 detection
management_steps.caution: Never rely on a single confirmation method

management_steps.details: Note and document ETT depth
Repeat BP and check all vital signs, Chest X-Ray, ABG after 30 minutes, Sedation if required (Morphine/Midazolam - watch BP)
Attach ventilator with HME Filter

drugs.notes: Indication: Induction. Preparation: 10mg/ml. Onset: <30sec. Duration: 5-10min. Caution: Hypotension, Soybean/egg allergy - Reduce dose with low BP
Indication: Induction. Preparation: 2mg/ml. Onset: <30sec. Duration: 5-10min. Caution: Adrenal suppression - Lesser effect on BP
Indication: Induction. Preparation: 10/50/100mg/ml. Onset: 30-60sec. Duration: 15-20min. Caution: IHD, Increased HR/BP - Useful for bronchospasm
Indication: Paralysis. Preparation: 50mg/ml. Onset: 45-60sec. Duration: 5-10min. Caution: Increased K+, Denervated muscle, Crush Injury - Repeat dosing may need pre-treatment with atropine
Indication: Paralysis. Preparation: 10mg/ml. Onset: 60-90sec. Duration: 40-60min. Caution: Difficult BVM - Alternate to Succinylcholine

Indication: Rescue for post-intubation hypotension. Preparation: Dilute 1amp (1000mcg) with 9ml NS (100mcg/ml), then dilute 1ml of that solution with another 9ml NS = 10mcg/ml. Onset: <1min. Duration: 5-10min. Caution: IHD

difficult_airway.prediction: LEMON criteria: L-Look externally, E-Evaluate 3-3-2 rule (mouth opening 3 fingers, chin-to-hyoid 3 fingers, hyoid-to-thyroid 2 fingers), M-Mallampati score, O-Obesity/Obstruction, N-Neck mobility, S-Saturation
setup: Prepare all equipment and staff beforehand if time allows
Video laryngoscope prepared if not initial device
Difficult/surgical airway box available (top shelf in resus)
Double setup - 1 staff member to intubate, 1 for surgical airway scrubbed and ready
Mark cricothyroid membrane with permanent marker prior to induction
Consider DSI (delayed sequence intubation) and vocal cord visualization prior to muscle relaxation
optimization: Consider awake or nasal intubation - experienced operator ONLY
special_considerations: Anaphylaxis and angioedema: LMA unlikely to be effective. Consider early surgical airway
UGIB/Haemoptysis: Consider SALAD technique. DL may offer better views than VL due to blood on fibre-optic scope
Stridor: LMA unlikely to be effective. Consider early surgical airway. Early ENT consultation
Ludwig's Angina/Retropharyngeal Abscess: Avoid airway manipulation if possible. Likely to require formal surgical airway in theatre

surgical_airway_cricothyrotomy.notes: Emergency surgical airway kit located in difficult airway box on top shelf in resus. Refer to Difficult Airway & Cricothyrotomy protocols.

management_steps.details: Shout for nearby help, activate emergency response system via mobile device, get AED and emergency equipment
Look for no breathing or only gasping and check pulse simultaneously. Is pulse definitely felt within 10 seconds?

management_steps.action: If normal breathing and pulse felt
management_steps.details: Monitor until emergency responders arrive

management_steps.action: If no normal breathing but pulse felt
management_steps.details: Provide rescue breathing: 1 breath every 6 seconds (10 breaths/min). Check pulse every 2 minutes; if no pulse, start CPR. If possible opioid overdose, administer naloxone if available per protocol.

management_steps.action: If no breathing or only gasping and pulse not felt
management_steps.details: Start CPR: Perform cycles of 30 compressions and 2 breaths. Use AED as soon as available.
management_steps.caution: Push hard (at least 2 inches/5cm) and fast (100-120/min), allow complete chest recoil, minimize interruptions

management_steps.details: Shockable rhythm: Give 1 shock, resume CPR immediately for 2 minutes. Non-shockable: Resume CPR immediately for 2 minutes.
management_steps.caution: Continue until ALS providers take over or victim starts to move

settings.conditions: Once advanced airway in place, continuous compressions

management_steps.details: Give oxygen, attach monitor/defibrillator
VF/pVT = Shockable. Asystole/PEA = Non-shockable
CPR 2 min, IV/IO access
If still shockable: Shock again, CPR 2 min, Epinephrine every 3-5 min, consider advanced airway and capnography
Give epinephrine ASAP, then every 3-5 min. Consider advanced airway. H's and T's.
Continue cycles. If ROSC: Post-cardiac arrest care.

warnings: Double sequential defibrillation may be considered for refractory VF
protocols.disposition: ROSC: Post-cardiac arrest care. No ROSC after 20 min: Consider terminating resuscitation.
protocols.reversible_causes_hs_ts: H's: Hypoxia, Hypovolaemia, Hydrogen ions (acidosis), Hyperkalaemia/Hypokalaemia, Hypothermia, Hypoglycaemia | T's: Tension pneumothorax, Tamponade (cardiac), Toxins, Thrombosis (pulmonary/coronary), Trauma

classification.name: Generalised tonic-clonic
classification.criteria: Generalised convulsions with loss of consciousness

classification.name: Focal
classification.criteria: Localised seizure activity, may progress to generalised

classification.name: Absence
classification.criteria: Brief loss of awareness, no convulsions

classification.name: Status epilepticus
classification.criteria: Seizure >5 min or multiple seizures without recovery between

management_steps.details: Airway, breathing, circulation. Place patient in recovery position. Protect from injury. Do NOT restrain.
management_steps.caution: Do NOT put anything in mouth

management_steps.details: If glucose <3 mmol/L: Give 50ml 50% dextrose IV (adults) or 2ml/kg 25% dextrose (children)
IV Lorazepam 4mg (adults) or 0.1mg/kg (children). If no IV access: IM Midazolam 10mg (adults) or Buccal Midazolam 0.5mg/kg (children). Repeat once after 5-10 min if seizure continues.
If seizure continues after 2 benzodiazepine doses: Phenytoin 20mg/kg IV at max 50mg/min OR Levetiracetam 60mg/kg IV (max 4.5g)
If seizure continues: Intubate and ventilate. Propofol or Midazolam infusion. ICU admission.
Bloods: glucose, electrolytes, calcium, magnesium, LFTs, ammonia, anticonvulsant levels, FBC. CT brain. LP if fever/infection suspected and CT normal.

drugs.notes: First-line for status epilepticus. Repeat once after 5-10 min if needed.
Second-line. Monitor BP and cardiac rhythm.

warnings: Do NOT put anything in the patient's mouth during seizure
Monitor for respiratory depression with benzodiazepines
Status epilepticus >5 minutes requires urgent treatment
protocols.disposition: Status epilepticus: ICU admission. Single seizure with known cause and full recovery: May discharge with follow-up.
protocols.reversible_causes: Hypoglycaemia, Hypoxia, Hypontaemia, Hypocalcaemia, Hypomagnesaemia, Infection (CNS), Trauma, Toxins, Stroke, Tumour, Withdrawal (alcohol/benzodiazepines)

management_steps.details: First-line access. 2 large bore (14-16G) IV lines for resuscitation. Upper limbs preferred.
management_steps.caution: Avoid veins over joints if possible

management_steps.details: If no peripheral access within 60-90 seconds in critically ill patient. Any age. Proximal tibia, distal tibia, proximal humerus.
management_steps.caution: Contraindicated if fracture/infection at site, previous IO in same bone

management_steps.details: Internal jugular, subclavian, or femoral. Ultrasound guided preferred. For: CVP monitoring, vasopressors, TPN, difficult access.
management_steps.caution: Contraindications: Infection at site, thrombosis, coagulopathy (relative)

management_steps.details: Confirm placement (CXR for CVC, aspiration/flush for IO), secure, document.
management_steps.caution: IO: Monitor for extravasation, compartment syndrome

warnings: IO is NOT for prophylactic use - only when peripheral access fails
IO needles should be removed within 24 hours
Monitor IO site for extravasation and compartment syndrome

classification.name: Synchronised cardioversion
classification.criteria: For tachyarrhythmias with pulse (AF, atrial flutter, SVT, VT with pulse)

management_steps.details: Consent if awake. Sedation if required. Sync button ON for synchronised cardioversion.
Narrow regular: 50-100J. Narrow irregular: 120-200J. Wide regular: 100J. Wide irregular: 200J (defibrillation dose).
Charge defibrillator. Clear team. Deliver shock.
Check pulse and rhythm. Monitor BP. If unsuccessful, increase energy and repeat.

warnings: Ensure synchronisation for all shockable rhythms with pulse
protocols.disposition: Monitor in resus after cardioversion. Admit to ICU/CCU if unstable.
